reviews.review_msg: Nicky is a chill prof who makes the class engaging. The creative project is fun, and she preps you well for the exam. I found unstuck study super helpful for study prep too.

I took ENGL113W and loved it. Nicky is an excellent professor; very laid-back and clearly cares for her students. The texts are not very difficult and the creative project is great for anyone who likes theatre/scriptwriting. She prepares the class well for the exam, just pay attention and it'll be smooth sailing.
Nicky was so delightful. Funny and she knows what she is talking about. Our class was mostly self marked, and we could choose whether or not to be marked on participation.

Nicky fosters discussion quite well. I normally never participate in class discussion, but I really felt inclined to with Nicky. All of her assignments were engaging and had many options to be as applicable as possible to the student. The late penalty was pretty lax, with only a 1% deduction per day late. Overall cool prof, if you like English.

As an intended English major, this class made me change my mind. It is extremely uncommon that the lecture itself is useful. The texts are interesting enough, and the content is alright, but the lectures are literally Nicky talking for 10 mins and the rest is opinions from peers which suck. She seems nice but save yourself the despair.

Nicky is one of the best professors I have ever had. She is kind, compassionate, and super funny too. I took this class for the Q credit but left throughly surprised at how much fun I had. Definitely talk to her one on one because she is super accommodating and goes out of her way to help you succeed. I look forward to taking more courses with her!

Nicky did not even pretend to care about the wellbeing of her students in the middle of a global pandemic. Grading criteria was never explained and instructions were always unclear. For the most part, you had to teach yourself. This course had so much potential but was one of the worst courses I've ever taken. Avoid this prof at all costs.

I'm an English major, and I really did not like Nicky's class. Her expectations were unclear, and despite this, she still marked hard. She gave me a terrible mark on my project for no reason, and when I asked her to explain why, she couldn't.

Such a kind prof! I very much enjoyed her class, despite English being one of my worst subjects. She was very engaging and you could tell that she cared a lot about her students. The books read for the semester were very interesting and I learned a lot about life and myself in this class.

i loved having dr. didicher as a prof! these negative reviews are so crazy to me, because i'm in my 3rd yr. now and it's safe to say that she's hands down one of the best teachers at sfu. she's really caring towards her students and extremely passionate. people say she's a tough marker but i didn't really feel that at all. 10/10
